headline: 46 contracts catalogued across 24 chains; 3 core contracts (token, vault, proxy-admin) and 43 protocol instances; upgradeability confirmed via TransparentUpgradeableProxy

findings.text: Etherscan block-explorer name-tag search for uniBTC surfaced primary token and vault contracts on Ethereum, confirmed via official docs page showing comprehensive chain support
Official deployments documentation page at docs.bedrock.technology/security/smart-contracts/unibtc lists 24 chains with uniBTC token and Vault contract addresses; documented as of 2024-11-21

GitHub repo contains audit PDFs: PeckShield-Audit-Report-uniBTC-v1.0.pdf, blocksec_bedrock_unibtc_v1.0-signed.pdf, blocksec_bedrock_unibtc_v1.1-signed.pdf; audit PDFs list scope of token and vault contracts

GitHub repo Bedrock-Technology/uniBTC contains deployments/ directory referenced as source; uniBTC.sol at main branch shows Initializable, ERC20Upgradeable, AccessControlUpgradeable pattern confirming upgradeable architecture

Multi-chain enumeration: 24 chains confirmed (Bitcoin, Ethereum, Merlin, BOB, Binance, Mantle, Arbitrum, Base, Bitlayer, BSquared, Optimism, Mode, Hemi, RSK, TAC, Taiko, ZetaChain, Berachain, Corn, Sonic, Sei, Duckchain, UniChain, Aptos, Solana, HyperEVM, Ink, X Layer); each has independent contract instances per chain
No factory pattern detected; uniBTC is a token contract, not a factory generating child contracts. Each chain deployment is a standalone token/vault pair

Identified 3 core administrative contracts: (1) uniBTC token (TransparentUpgradeableProxy) on each chain, (2) Vault (TransparentUpgradeableProxy) on each chain, (3) ProxyAdmin contract (Ownable) managing proxy upgrades on Ethereum; role taxonomy: token, vault, proxy_admin
All catalogued addresses sourced from verified docs.bedrock.technology, Etherscan verified contracts, and GitHub repository with identified commit references

rationale.verdict: Discovery slice: documented addresses across 24 chains from official deployment docs, Etherscan verification, and GitHub audit artifacts. No grade assignment for discovery; downstream slices will evaluate admin roles and control structures.

evidence.shows: Official uniBTC deployment addresses table listing token and vault contracts for 24 chains including Ethereum, BNB Chain, Arbitrum, Optimism, Base, BOB, Mantle, Mode, Merlin, Bitlayer, B² Network, Taiko, ZetaChain, Rootstock, Berachain, Sonic, Sei, Duckchain, Hemi, TAC, UniChain, Aptos, Solana, HyperEVM, Ink, X Layer

Ethereum mainnet uniBTC token verified as TransparentUpgradeableProxy with logic implementation at 0x51A7f889480c57cbeea81614f7D0bE2B70db6c5e and proxy admin at 0x029E4FbDAa31DE075dD74B2238222A08233978f6; uses EIP-1967 pattern
ProxyAdmin contract (Ownable) at 0x029E4FbDAa31DE075dD74B2238222A08233978f6 deployed by 0x9251fd3d79522bb2243a58fff1db43e25a495aab on 2024-05-22; manages proxy upgrades for uniBTC token

Ethereum uniBTC Vault contract verified as TransparentUpgradeableProxy with implementation at 0x01E9161d1621466eb086651fd514d3efb8c3752e; holds BTC-derivative assets (FBTC, WBTC, cbBTC, BBTC); created by 0x899c284a89e113056a72dc9ade5b60e80dd3c94f

GitHub repository with uniBTC.sol source code showing Initializable, ERC20Upgradeable, ERC20BurnableUpgradeable, AccessControlUpgradeable; includes audit PDFs (PeckShield v1.0, BlockSec v1.0 and v1.1) and deployments directory referencing chain-specific addresses
Official Bedrock protocol website confirming uniBTC is restaking solution for wBTC holders; references Babylon Bitcoin staking protocol and multi-chain deployment

unknowns: D6-offchain: No factory enumeration performed; uniBTC is token-only, not factory-based; if factory pattern exists for Vault pools, cap at 50 child instances per D6 rules
D4-offchain: GitHub /deployments directory fetch blocked by permissions; deployment artifacts referenced but not directly validated via JSON broadcast files

protocol_metadata.about: uniBTC is a restaking solution enabling wBTC holders to earn staking rewards through partnerships with Bitcoin staking protocols like Babylon without redeeming their wrapped BTC tokens. The protocol accepts wrapped BTC assets and deploys them across multiple yield-generating protocols, offering multi-chain support for seamless DeFi integration while maintaining Ethereum-level security standards.
